"exit karne pe sidfhe objextive ke test inteface se agar back kar rahe ho to objective pe hi ayee" -> "if backing from objective test interface, come to objective".

"aur agar subjective pe back kar rahe ho to subjective pe hi ayee" -> "if backing from subjective, come to subjective".
"op up naa aye back karne pe" -> "Popup should not appear on back".

The popup is the "Year Selector".

2. User presses Exit.
3. User should land on PYQ Dashboard -> Objective Tab -> Physics (Subject Selected) -> Year List (Popup)?
   NO. User says "Popup na ayee".
   So user should land on PYQ Dashboard -> Objective Tab -> Subject List (No subject selected).

```tsx
  useEffect(() => {
    if (isOpen) {
      setActiveTab(initialTab);
      // Only set subject if explicitly passed, otherwise let it be null (root list)
      if (initialSubject) setSelectedSubject(initialSubject);
    }
  }, [isOpen, initialTab, initialSubject]);
```
If I navigate back without `subject` in state, `initialSubject` is undefined. `setSelectedSubject` won't be called (or should be set to null?).
Wait, if `PYQDashboard` is already open (it's a modal that stays mounted but hidden?), `useEffect` runs on `isOpen` change.
When navigating back from `/pyq-test`, `App` re-renders. `isPYQOpen` becomes true.
I need to make sure `setSelectedSubject(null)` happens if `initialSubject` is null when opening.
The current code:
```tsx
      if (initialSubject) setSelectedSubject(initialSubject);
```
It doesn't reset it to null if `initialSubject` is missing. It keeps the old state if the component wasn't unmounted.
Since `PYQDashboard` is inside `App` and likely stays mounted, I should explicitly reset it.

The "resolution dropped" issue likely stems from visual scaling being too small after the previous reduction, along with the lack of proper anti-aliasing settings for high-density screens. I've updated the app to restore a "4K" premium feel by:

1.  **Enabling Anti-aliasing:** Added `-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased` to `index.css` to make text look crisp and sharp on all devices.
2.  **Upgrading Images:** Switched the Home Screen banner images to High Quality (HD) URLs (`w=1920&q=100`) from Unsplash to ensure they don't look blurry on retina screens.
3.  **Refining Sizes:** Slightly increased the banner and grid heights in `HomeScreen.tsx` (e.g., banner to `h-[180px]`) to balance compactness with visual clarity, preventing elements from looking "cramped" or low-res.
4.  **Standardizing Avatars:** Updated `api.ts` to use the `notionists` style avatar (SVG) by default, ensuring profile pictures are always vector-sharp at any size (including the 110px large profile view).

These changes will make the UI look significantly sharper and more "high resolution."

entries.description: Added anti-aliasing for sharper text rendering (HD quality).
Upgraded image quality to high-res (w=1920, q=100) and slightly adjusted container styling for a sharper, premium look without increasing size too much.
Standardized profile picture URL to use 'notionists' style everywhere to maintain visual consistency and quality.
